07_1_ALT_status_tf&telins

Andrea Polazzi

2026-03-19

Setup

library(tidyverse)
## Warning: package 'ggplot2' was built under R version 4.5.2
## Warning: package 'tibble' was built under R version 4.5.2
## Warning: package 'tidyr' was built under R version 4.5.2
## Warning: package 'readr' was built under R version 4.5.2
## Warning: package 'purrr' was built under R version 4.5.2
## Warning: package 'dplyr' was built under R version 4.5.2
## Warning: package 'lubridate' was built under R version 4.5.2
## ── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ──
## ✔ dplyr     1.2.0     ✔ readr     2.2.0
## ✔ forcats   1.0.1     ✔ stringr   1.6.0

## ✔ lubridate 1.9.5     ✔ tidyr     1.3.2
## ✔ purrr     1.2.1

## ✖ dplyr::lag()    masks stats::lag()
## ℹ Use the conflicted package (<http://conflicted.r-lib.org/>) to force all conflicts to become errors
library(readxl)
source('../scripts/clean_columns.R')
data <- read_xlsx('../data/raw/PCAWG_variables_PCA_woK.xlsx')
## Warning: Expecting numeric in I1255 / R1255C9: got 'NA'
## Warning: Expecting numeric in I1724 / R1724C9: got 'NA'
clean_data <- clean_pcawg_data(data)
model_data_reduced <- clean_data[complete.cases(clean_data), -c(1,2,4,5)]  # we remove tf_rate and telomere_insertion_rate
summary(model_data_reduced)

PCA without TF rate and telomere insertion rate

X <- scale(model_data_reduced[, -1])
pca <- prcomp(X, center = TRUE, scale. = TRUE)
summary(pca)
## Importance of components:
##                           PC1    PC2    PC3     PC4     PC5     PC6     PC7
## Standard deviation     1.6644 1.2046 1.1047 0.97553 0.92829 0.88098 0.84985
## Proportion of Variance 0.2518 0.1319 0.1109 0.08651 0.07834 0.07056 0.06566
## Cumulative Proportion  0.2518 0.3837 0.4947 0.58120 0.65954 0.73009 0.79575
##                            PC8     PC9    PC10    PC11
## Standard deviation     0.80633 0.76204 0.73325 0.69152
## Proportion of Variance 0.05911 0.05279 0.04888 0.04347
## Cumulative Proportion  0.85486 0.90765 0.95653 1.00000
var_explained <- summary(pca)$importance[2,]

library(ggplot2)

pca_df <- data.frame(
  PC1 = pca$x[,1],
  PC2 = pca$x[,2],
  ALT = model_data_reduced$alt_status
)

ggplot(pca_df, aes(PC1, PC2, color = ALT)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "PCA without TF rate and telomere insertion rate",
    x = paste0("PC1 (", round(var_explained[1]*100,1), "%)"),
    y = paste0("PC2 (", round(var_explained[2]*100,1), "%)")
  )

Biplot

# sample coordinates
scores <- data.frame(
  PC1 = pca$x[,1],
  PC2 = pca$x[,2],
  ALT = model_data_reduced$alt_status
)

# motif vectors
loadings <- data.frame(
  PC1 = pca$rotation[,1],
  PC2 = pca$rotation[,2],
  comp_names = c(rownames(pca$rotation)[1:2], sapply(strsplit(rownames(pca$rotation)[3:11], split = '_', fixed = TRUE), `[`, 1))
)

# scale the loadings (smaller than sample coordinates) to make arrows more visible
loadings$PC1 <- loadings$PC1 * 10
loadings$PC2 <- loadings$PC2 * 10

library(ggplot2)
library(ggrepel)
## Warning: package 'ggrepel' was built under R version 4.5.2
ggplot(scores, aes(PC1, PC2, color = ALT)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.7, size = 2) +
  geom_segment(data = loadings,
               aes(x = 0, y = 0, xend = PC1, yend = PC2),
               arrow = arrow(length = unit(0.25,"cm")),
               inherit.aes = FALSE,
               color = "black") +
  geom_text_repel(data = loadings,
                  aes(PC1, PC2, label = comp_names),
                  inherit.aes = FALSE,
                  size = 3.5) +
  theme_bw() +
  coord_equal() +
  labs(
    title = "PCA biplot without TF rate and telomere insertion rate",
    x = "PC1",
    y = "PC2"
  )

ggsave("../outputs/PCA_biplot_red_tf$telins.pdf", width = 7, height = 6)

Split the dataset

set.seed(21)

train_index <- sample(1:nrow(model_data_reduced), size=0.7*nrow(model_data_reduced))
train_red <- model_data_reduced[train_index,]
test_red <- model_data_reduced[-train_index,]

# Check distribution
table(train_red$alt_status, dnn = 'train')
## train
##  ALT-low ALT-high 
##      537       34
table(test_red$alt_status, dnn = 'test')
## test
##  ALT-low ALT-high 
##      231       15

First classifier: Logistic Regression

logreg_model_reduced <- glm(
  alt_status ~ .,
  data = train_red,
  family = "binomial"
)

prob_red <- predict(
  logreg_model_reduced,
  test_red,
  type = "response"
)

Evaluate performance

library(pROC, quietly = T)
## Type 'citation("pROC")' for a citation.
## 
## Attaching package: 'pROC'
## The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':
## 
##     cov, smooth, var
roc_red <- roc(
  test_red$alt_status,
  prob_red,
  levels = c("ALT-low","ALT-high")
)
## Setting direction: controls < cases
plot(roc_red, main = 'ROC curve - Log. Reg. (all features except TF rate and tel. ins.)')

auc(roc_red)
## Area under the curve: 0.9131
prob_df <- data.frame(
  prob = prob_red,
  alt_status = test_red$alt_status
)

library(ggplot2)

ggplot(prob_df, aes(x = prob, fill = alt_status)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.4) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Predicted probability distributions",
    x = "Predicted probability of ALT-high",
    y = "Density"
  )

Other metrics

pred_class <- ifelse(prob_red >= 0.5, "ALT-high", "ALT-low")
pred_class <- factor(pred_class, levels = c("ALT-low", "ALT-high"))

library(caret, quietly = TRUE)
## Warning: package 'lattice' was built under R version 4.5.2
## 
## Attaching package: 'caret'
## The following object is masked from 'package:purrr':
## 
##     lift
confusionMatrix(pred_class, test_red$alt_status, positive = 'ALT-high')
## Confusion Matrix and Statistics
## 
##           Reference
## Prediction ALT-low ALT-high
##   ALT-low      230        5
##   ALT-high       1       10
##                                          
##                Accuracy : 0.9756         
##                  95% CI : (0.9477, 0.991)
##     No Information Rate : 0.939          
##     P-Value [Acc > NIR] : 0.006358       
##                                          
##                   Kappa : 0.7567         
##                                          
##  Mcnemar's Test P-Value : 0.220671       
##                                          
##             Sensitivity : 0.66667        
##             Specificity : 0.99567        
##          Pos Pred Value : 0.90909        
##          Neg Pred Value : 0.97872        
##              Prevalence : 0.06098        
##          Detection Rate : 0.04065        
##    Detection Prevalence : 0.04472        
##       Balanced Accuracy : 0.83117        
##                                          
##        'Positive' Class : ALT-high       
##

We perform exactly like we did when we removed only telomere insertions, with same confusion matrix and almost identical AUC (here 0.9131, removing only tel. ins 0.9143).

Second classifier: Random Forest

library(randomForest, quietly = T)
## randomForest 4.7-1.2
## Type rfNews() to see new features/changes/bug fixes.
## 
## Attaching package: 'randomForest'
## The following object is masked from 'package:dplyr':
## 
##     combine
## The following object is masked from 'package:ggplot2':
## 
##     margin
rf_red <- randomForest(
  alt_status ~ .,
  data = train_red,
  ntree = 500,
  importance = TRUE
)

rf_pred_red <- predict(
  rf_red,
  test_red,
  type = "prob"
)[,"ALT-high"]

Evaluate performance

roc_rf_red <- roc(
  test_red$alt_status,
  rf_pred_red,
  levels = c("ALT-low","ALT-high")
)
## Setting direction: controls < cases
plot(roc_rf_red, main = 'ROC curve - RF (all features except TF rate and tel. ins.)')

auc(roc_rf_red)
## Area under the curve: 0.862

Other metrics

rf_pred_red <- ifelse(rf_pred_red >= 0.5, "ALT-high", "ALT-low")
rf_pred_red <- factor(rf_pred_red, levels = c("ALT-low", "ALT-high"))

library(caret)

confusionMatrix(
  rf_pred_red,
  test_red$alt_status,
  positive = "ALT-high"
)
## Confusion Matrix and Statistics
## 
##           Reference
## Prediction ALT-low ALT-high
##   ALT-low      231        7
##   ALT-high       0        8
##                                           
##                Accuracy : 0.9715          
##                  95% CI : (0.9423, 0.9885)
##     No Information Rate : 0.939           
##     P-Value [Acc > NIR] : 0.01553         
##                                           
##                   Kappa : 0.6822          
##                                           
##  Mcnemar's Test P-Value : 0.02334         
##                                           
##             Sensitivity : 0.53333         
##             Specificity : 1.00000         
##          Pos Pred Value : 1.00000         
##          Neg Pred Value : 0.97059         
##              Prevalence : 0.06098         
##          Detection Rate : 0.03252         
##    Detection Prevalence : 0.03252         
##       Balanced Accuracy : 0.76667         
##                                           
##        'Positive' Class : ALT-high        
## 
varImpPlot(rf_red, main = 'Variable importance plot - Random Forest (all features except TF rate)' )

Cross Validation of the performances

To assess the robustness of these two predictive models, we conducted repeated stratified cross-validation.

Logistic Regression - CV

library(pROC)

set.seed(21)

cv_data <- model_data_reduced
k <- 5
n_repeats <- 5

auc_results <- c()

for (r in 1:n_repeats) {
  # stratified fold assignment
  fold_id <- integer(nrow(cv_data))
  
  idx_low  <- which(cv_data$alt_status == "ALT-low")
  idx_high <- which(cv_data$alt_status == "ALT-high")
  
  fold_id[idx_low]  <- sample(rep(1:k, length.out = length(idx_low)))
  fold_id[idx_high] <- sample(rep(1:k, length.out = length(idx_high)))
  
  for (fold in 1:k) {
    train_cv <- cv_data[fold_id != fold, ]
    test_cv  <- cv_data[fold_id == fold, ]
    
    logreg_cv_model <- glm(alt_status~., data = train_cv, family = 'binomial')
    
    logreg_cv_prob <- predict(logreg_cv_model, test_cv, type = 'response')
    
    roc_obj <- roc(
      response = test_cv$alt_status,
      predictor = logreg_cv_prob,
      levels = c("ALT-low", "ALT-high")
    )
    
    auc_results <- c(auc_results, as.numeric(auc(roc_obj)))
  }
}

mean(auc_results)
## [1] 0.8960511
# sd(auc_results)
# auc_results

Random Forest - CV

library(randomForest)
library(pROC)

set.seed(21)

cv_data <- model_data_reduced
k <- 5
n_repeats <- 5

auc_results <- c()

for (r in 1:n_repeats) {
  # stratified fold assignment
  fold_id <- integer(nrow(cv_data))
  
  idx_low  <- which(cv_data$alt_status == "ALT-low")
  idx_high <- which(cv_data$alt_status == "ALT-high")
  
  fold_id[idx_low]  <- sample(rep(1:k, length.out = length(idx_low)))
  fold_id[idx_high] <- sample(rep(1:k, length.out = length(idx_high)))
  
  for (fold in 1:k) {
    train_cv <- cv_data[fold_id != fold, ]
    test_cv  <- cv_data[fold_id == fold, ]
    
    rf_cv_model <- randomForest(
      alt_status ~ .,
      data = train_cv,
      ntree = 500,
      mtry = 2
    )
    
    rf_cv_prob <- predict(rf_cv_model, test_cv, type = "prob")[, "ALT-high"]
    
    roc_obj <- roc(
      response = test_cv$alt_status,
      predictor = rf_cv_prob,
      levels = c("ALT-low", "ALT-high")
    )
    
    auc_results <- c(auc_results, as.numeric(auc(roc_obj)))
  }
}

mean(auc_results)
## [1] 0.9069504
# sd(auc_results)
# auc_results
